agenda.description: Table of contents listing deck sections.
agenda.goal: After executive summary, before first content section
guidelines: 3-6 agenda items
Each item: section number + title + optional description
Action title describes what the agenda covers (not just 'Agenda')
when_to_use: After executive summary, before first content section
To set audience expectations for deck structure

op_recipe.note: each agenda item as positioned text, vertically stacked with consistent spacing

agenda.template_layout_hint: Title Only layout with positioned shapes

bar_chart.description: Bar or column chart comparing values across categories.
bar_chart.goal: Comparing values across categories (revenue by segment, cost by department)
guidelines: Sort by value (largest first) unless chronological
Data labels on bars for readability
Legend at bottom for multi-series; hide for single series
Round numbers (use ~$2.5B not $2,487,392,104)
One chart per slide
when_to_use: Comparing values across categories (revenue by segment, cost by department)
Showing change over discrete time periods
Ranking items by value

op_recipe.note: role: title. Fallback: add_text if no title placeholder
categories, series, position from layout geometry
source line at bottom of slide, small font

bar_chart.template_layout_hint: Title Only layout, chart as positioned shape

big_number.description: Highlight a single metric with large typography.
big_number.goal: When one number tells the story (revenue, growth rate, market size)
guidelines: Number: large, bold, prominent
Label: small text below explaining what the number represents
Optional context line or comparison
when_to_use: When one number tells the story (revenue, growth rate, market size)
Impact slides

op_recipe.note: label/context below the number, smaller font

big_statement.description: Large-text emphasis slide for a single powerful message.
big_statement.goal: Key takeaway or provocative statement that needs visual weight
guidelines: One sentence, large font, centered
No bullets, no charts — just the message
when_to_use: Key takeaway or provocative statement that needs visual weight
Transition moments in the narrative

op_recipe.note: large centered text with explicit coordinates

big_statement.visual_hint: Large message on full colored background

content_bullets.description: Slide with action title and bulleted body text.
content_bullets.goal: Qualitative arguments, lists of findings, recommendations
guidelines: Action title states the insight
3-6 bullet points, parallel structure
Use bold lead-ins for scanability
Avoid walls of text — if > 6 bullets, split into two slides
when_to_use: Qualitative arguments, lists of findings, recommendations
When the message is text-based, not data-driven

op_recipe.note: role: body, text: bulleted text. Fallback: set_placeholder_text or add_text

content_text.description: Narrative text slide for longer-form explanations.
content_text.goal: When a narrative paragraph is more appropriate than bullets
guidelines: Action title states the insight
Body: 1-3 short paragraphs, not bullets
Keep concise — if it needs scrolling, split the slide
when_to_use: When a narrative paragraph is more appropriate than bullets
Context-setting slides, methodology descriptions

op_recipe.note: role: body. Fallback: set_placeholder_text or add_text

disclaimer.goal: End of deck or where legally required
guidelines: Small text, standard legal language
Minimal visual styling
when_to_use: End of deck or where legally required

end_slide.description: Closing slide (thank you, contact info, next steps).
guidelines: Keep minimal — title or thank-you message
Optional: contact details or next-steps summary
when_to_use: Last slide of the deck

op_recipe.note: title: closing message, subtitle: contact or next steps

end_slide.template_layout_hint: End layout or Title Slide layout
end_slide.visual_hint: Close with ownership and next action

executive_summary.description: Top-level summary stating the deck's key recommendation.
executive_summary.goal: Early in the deck (slide 2-3) to state the answer up front
guidelines: Action title states the core recommendation
Body: 3-5 bullet points covering situation, complication, and key arguments
This is the most important slide — reader should get the full message here
when_to_use: Early in the deck (slide 2-3) to state the answer up front
Use SCQA: action title = Answer, body = Situation + Complication + supporting points

op_recipe.note: role: title, text: action title (the Answer)
role: body, text: bullet points. Fallback: set_placeholder_text or add_text

executive_summary.template_layout_hint: Title and Text or content layout with body placeholder

guidelines: Keep content brief — space is tight
Equal column widths, parallel structure
when_to_use: Four workstreams, pillars, or phases
Frameworks with four components

op_recipe.note: column 1-4 content, each with explicit coordinates

green_panel_text.description: Colored panel with key message text.
green_panel_text.goal: Highlighting a critical finding or call to action
guidelines: Panel fills a significant portion of the slide
Text: concise, high-contrast against panel color
when_to_use: Highlighting a critical finding or call to action
Visual break with emphasis

op_recipe.note: layout with colored panel or Title Only
panel message text with explicit coordinates

green_panel_text.visual_hint: Colored anchor panel with structured text on the right

icon_grid.description: Grid of icons with labels for capability maps or feature lists.
icon_grid.goal: Capability overviews, service offerings, feature lists
guidelines: 3-6 items in a grid (2x2, 2x3, 3x3)
Each item: icon/emoji + short label + optional description
Consistent spacing and alignment
when_to_use: Capability overviews, service offerings, feature lists
When visual variety is needed to break up text-heavy sections

op_recipe.note: each grid item as positioned text box with label + description, arranged in grid pattern

line_chart.description: Line chart showing trends over time.
line_chart.goal: Time series data (monthly, quarterly, annual trends)
guidelines: X-axis: time periods, Y-axis: metric
2-5 series max — more becomes unreadable
Label end points directly when possible
when_to_use: Time series data (monthly, quarterly, annual trends)
Comparing trajectories across groups

op_recipe.note: role: title. Fallback: add_text if no title placeholder

matrix_2x2.description: 2x2 matrix for strategic positioning or categorization.
guidelines: Label both axes clearly
Name each quadrant
Place items as positioned text in the relevant quadrant
when_to_use: Strategic frameworks (growth-share, effort-impact, risk-reward)
Categorizing items along two dimensions

op_recipe.note: quadrant labels and items, positioned in each quadrant

pie_chart.description: Pie or doughnut chart showing composition/share.
pie_chart.goal: Market share, revenue mix, budget allocation
guidelines: Max 6 slices — group small ones into 'Other'
Label slices with percentage + category name
Consider doughnut for a cleaner look
when_to_use: Market share, revenue mix, budget allocation
When parts-of-a-whole is the message

op_recipe.note: role: title. Fallback: add_text if no title placeholder

guidelines: 3-6 steps — more needs a different format
Horizontal flow (left to right) preferred
Each step: short label + optional description
when_to_use: Implementation roadmaps, workflows, step-by-step processes
When order and sequence matter

op_recipe.note: each step as a positioned text box, arranged horizontally with consistent spacing

quote.goal: Customer testimonials, expert opinions, strategic framing
guidelines: Quote text: large, italic or styled
Attribution: smaller, below the quote
Do NOT use set_semantic_text role: title — quote layouts often lack title placeholders
when_to_use: Customer testimonials, expert opinions, strategic framing
When a voice from outside the team adds credibility

op_recipe.note: quote text with explicit coordinates, large font

section_divider.description: Visual break between major sections of the deck.
section_divider.goal: Before each major argument/section (for decks > 8 slides)
guidelines: Keep minimal — title only, no body content
Optional subtitle for section context
when_to_use: Before each major argument/section (for decks > 8 slides)
Title: section theme (not an action title)

op_recipe.note: title: section name, subtitle: optional context

guidelines: Header row: bold, distinct background
Max 6-8 rows, 4-6 columns — more needs an appendix
Align numbers right, text left
Highlight key cells with color
when_to_use: Detailed comparisons across multiple dimensions
When exact values matter more than visual pattern

op_recipe.note: role: title. Fallback: add_text if no title placeholder

table.template_layout_hint: Title Only or Title and Text layout

three_column.description: Three-column layout for parallel comparisons or categories.
guidelines: Equal column widths, parallel structure
Column headers: bold, descriptive
when_to_use: Three options, workstreams, or categories
When content is naturally parallel across three items

title_slide.description: Opening cover slide with deck title and subtitle.
guidelines: Title: deck name or key theme (not an action title)
Subtitle: date, audience, author, or context line
when_to_use: First slide of every deck

guidelines: Column headers: bold, descriptive
Parallel content structure across columns
when_to_use: Before/after, pros/cons, current vs. proposed
Two parallel arguments or perspectives

op_recipe.note: left column: header + content, explicit left/top/width
right column: header + content, explicit left/top/width

two_column.template_layout_hint: Two Content layout or Title Only with positioned shapes
